title: To continue Executive Order 14220 in effect indefinitely.
plain_summary: This bill makes a special government rule about copper imports stay in place forever. The rule is called Executive Order 14220 and it deals with national security issues related to bringing copper into the United States. Right now this rule would normally end at some point, but this bill would make it permanent. All the government actions and decisions that came from this rule would also stay in place forever.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Executive Order 14220
sections.text: Executive Order 14220 was signed on February twenty-fifth, two thousand twenty-five. This order is called Addressing the Threat to National Security From Imports of Copper. It was published in volume ninety of the Federal Register on page eleven thousand and one. This bill says that Executive Order 14220 must stay in effect forever. Any actions taken by government agencies because of this executive order must also stay in effect forever. Any rules or regulations that government agencies created because of this executive order must also stay in effect forever.
